sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_paths (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, path TEXT NOT NULL);
SELECT id, parse_dirname(path, 'system') AS dirname FROM test_paths WHERE parse_dirname(path, 'both_slash') | 1 = 1;
SELECT id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_paths tp WHERE parse_dirname(tp.path, 'both_slash') = parse_dirname(test_paths.path, 'both_slash')) AS top_dir_count FROM test_paths;
SELECT id, parse_dirname(path, 'forward_slash') AS top_dir, (parse_dirname(path, 'backslash') | LENGTH(path)) AS complex_expression FROM test_paths GROUP BY top_dir ORDER BY top_dir;

WITH path_counts AS (SELECT parse_dirname(path, 'backslash') AS top_dir, COUNT(*) AS count FROM test_paths GROUP BY top_dir HAVING count > 1) SELECT top_dir, count | 10 AS adjusted_count FROM path_counts;
CREATE TABLE number_tests (num1 INTEGER, num2 INTEGER);
SELECT num1 | num2 AS bitwise_result, (NUMERIC '0.12345678901234567890' | num1) AS coerced_result FROM number_tests WHERE num1 IS NOT NULL OR num2 IS NULL;
SELECT id, (parse_dirname(path, 'both_slash') | LENGTH(path)) AS mixed_expression FROM test_paths WHERE LENGTH(parse_dirname(path, 'system')) > 0;
SELECT path, num1, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM number_tests nt WHERE nt.num1 | nt.num2 < 50) FROM test_paths JOIN number_tests ON LENGTH(path) > 5;
